prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a skiv has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the skiv-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a skiv, a tarn, and a xav. The skiv is underneath the tarn. The tarn is to-the-left-of the xav. The skiv has a purple button on top, a red button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The tarn has a red button on top and a purple button on bottom. The xav has a blue button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the skiv activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a vro, a lurn, and a quan. The quan is underneath the lurn. The lurn is to-the-left-of the vro. The vro has a blue button on top and a red button on bottom. The lurn has a red button on top and a purple button on side. The quan has a purple button on top, a red button on side, and a blue button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the skiv-analog, and which button activates it?
